title: 10 best herbal teas for sunburn

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe teas helps with sunburn because they contain powerful antioxidants that help soothe and calm the skin. The soothing properties of aloe vera can reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation caused by sunburn, providing rapid relief from discomfort. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in aloe vera can help to accelerate the healing process, reducing the risk of blisters and peeling. As a result, drinking aloe tea can provide natural and effective relief from sunburned skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in aloe vera tea, including vitamins C and E, help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and accelerate the aging process, reducing the appearance of sun-damaged skin.

Sothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's gel and other compounds have a soothing and moisturizing effect on the skin, helping to calm irritation, reduce itchiness, and promote hydration, which aids in speeding up the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations caused by sunburn.
Leaves: Contain aloin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps to calm sunburned skin and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves or purchase high-quality dried aloe vera powder for your recipe.
Rinse the fresh aloe vera leaves thoroughly with cool water and remove the thick green skin from the outside.
Dry the inner gel of the fresh aloe vera leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 1 hour to dehydrate the liquid.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried aloe vera powder or 2 tablespoons of fresh aloe vera gel with 1 cup of boiling water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a glass and add 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 slice of lemon, serving warm or chilled as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. The tea's mucilages, such as glycosides and saponins, form a protective barrier on the skin's surface, calming heat and discomfort. Additionally, marshmallow tea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, further promoting healing and regeneration of sun-damaged skin. By quenching inflammation and promoting soothing relief, herbal marshmallow teas can provide quick and natural relief from painful sunburns.
remedy_properties: Emollient property: Marshmallow tea's emollient property helps to soothe and calm sunburned skin by forming a protective barrier that locks in moisture, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as mucilage and flavonoids, help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, promoting faster healing and relief.

Soothing property: The soothing properties of marshmallow tea, particularly its mucilage content, help to calm irritated skin and reduce itching, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving the discomfort of sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and cool sunburned skin.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried marshmallow root from a trusted source or online vendor weighing approximately one ounce.
Combine the harvested root with one quart of boiling water in a large glass pitcher allowing it steep for twenty minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container discarding the solids and reserving the infused liquid.
Add honey to taste dissolving two teaspoons of pure raw honey into the cooled tea while whisking gently.
Store the herbal marshmallow tea in an airtight glass container refrigerated for up to three days or frozen for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with sunburn because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory properties. The tea contains apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with sunburn. Additionally, the antioxidants present in pot marigold tea help to neutralize free radicals, which can cause further damage to the skin. When consumed as a tea, the anti-inflammatory compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream, providing relief from sunburned skin and promoting healthy recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold tea contains flavonoids like luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, can help neutralize free radicals generated by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and damage to the skin.
Soften and soothe property: Pot marigold tea contains saponins and mucilages that have been shown to soften and soothe irritated skin, making it an effective treatment for sunburn relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin.

Flowers: The flowers of the pot marigold plant contain flavonoids and carotenoids that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers from your garden or store-bought dried petals measure out one teaspoon for every cup of water.
Combine the calendula flowers with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into another container discard the solids and reserve the infused liquid.
Allow the infusion to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature which should take about 10-15 minutes.
Drink the pot marigold tea three times daily for up to five days or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort teas helps with sunburn because it contains hyperforin, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically or consumed as a tea, hyperforin can help reduce the redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburned skin. Additionally, St john's wort has been shown to accelerate the healing process by increasing blood flow and promoting collagen synthesis, leading to faster recovery from sun-damaged skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort tea contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by sunburn, promoting faster healing and reducing redness.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe sunburned skin and reduce pain, swelling, and discomfort.
Wound-healing properties: St John's Wort has been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve wound healing, which can help promote the natural repair process of sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioflavonoids and phenolic acids, which help soothe and calm skin irritation caused by sunburn.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers and rinse them gently under cool running water.
Combine one teaspoon of dried st john's wort or two teaspoons of fresh st john's wort with one cup boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot.
Let the tea cool slightly before drinking and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The antioxidants present in chamomile also help to neutralize free radicals caused by UV damage, promoting a faster recovery process. Furthermore, chamomile's gentle, cooling properties can ease the itchiness and stinging associated with sunburned skin, providing relief and comfort as the skin heals.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing skin damage and inflammation associated with sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sunburned skin.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have a direct soothing effect on the skin, providing quick relief from burning sensations, discomfort, and skin irritation caused by sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, a natural anti-inflammatory that reduces redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried chamomile flowers according to package instructions or fresh flowers from a trusted source.
Combine one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers or three teaspoons of fresh chamomile flowers with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor of the tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or cheesecloth to remove the herbs.
Drink the tea immediately after straining or let it cool to room temperature for added soothing benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The flavonoids present in licorice tea, such as glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburned skin. Additionally, the antioxidants found in licorice tea help to neutralize free radicals caused by UV damage, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further irritation. Drinking licorice tea regularly may also improve skin elasticity and hydration, further aiding in the recovery from sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburned skin.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in licorice tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and promoting skin healing.

Sothing and moisturizing properties: Licorice root contains mucilage, a natural thickening agent that provides a soothing and protective barrier on the skin, helping to calm and hydrate sunburned areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids that work as antioxidants to reduce redness and inflammation caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried licorice root or three grams of fresh licorice root.
Boil one cup of water and steep the licorice root in it for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well before serving the tea immediately after preparation.
Drink one to three cups of the herbal tea throughout the day as needed for relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with sunburn because of its soothing and calming properties. The menthol content in peppermint tea helps to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with sunburned skin. As you drink the tea, the menthol is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the skin, providing a cooling sensation that can help to calm redness and itching. Additionally, peppermint's antioxidant properties can also help to combat free radicals that can cause further damage to the skin. Overall, peppermint teas can provide quick relief from sunburn discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Peppermint tea's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in sunburned skin.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: The menthol content in peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, while also soothing the skin and promoting healing.

Cooling and Soothing Properties: Peppermint tea's cooling sensation helps to calm and relax the skin, reducing heat and discomfort caused by sunburn, while also providing a comforting and calming effect on the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of sun-damaged skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried peppermint leaves in amounts of one tablespoon per eight ounces water.
Bring filtered water to boiling point and pour it over the peppermint leaves in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes allowing the peppermint's flavonoids to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Drink the cooled peppermint tea immediately and repeat every two to three hours as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock teas helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea's active compounds, such as inulin and saponins, work to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by UV exposure. This helps to soothe and calm the skin, relieving symptoms like redness, itching, and discomfort. Additionally, burdock teas' cooling and calming effects can help to reduce heat and swelling associated with sunburn, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of further damage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as arctiin and luteolin, help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn, promoting faster healing.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in burdock tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting natural regeneration.

Cooling and soothing properties: Burdock tea's cooling and soothing effects, attributed to its mucilaginous compounds like inulin and fructooligosaccharides, help calm irritated sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and itching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain inulin, which helps soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and inflammation.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve pain and discomfort caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried burdock roots and flowers in a clean glass container.
Boil one teaspoon of dried burdock mixture in two cups of filtered water for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a large ceramic cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well before serving.
Let the tea steep for an additional 10-15 minutes to allow flavors to meld.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with sunburn because of its exceptional anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea's flavonoids, particularly quercetin, reduce redness and swelling by calming irritated skin cells. Meanwhile, vitamins A and C in the tea help soothe and hydrate the skin, promoting a faster recovery from sun damage. As the tea works to neutralize free radicals, it also helps prevent further inflammation and promotes healthy skin regeneration, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing sunburned skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Dandelion tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids in dandelion tea may help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Cooling and soothing properties: Dandelion's natural cooling properties, such as its ability to stimulate the production of prostaglandins, can help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing redness associated with sunburn.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves contain antioxidants and vitamins that help to calm and cool the skin, providing relief from sunburn discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried dandelion flowers measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Boil fresh water in a teapot or kettle for four to five minutes.
Add the measured dried dandelion flowers to the boiling water and steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat as needed up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling caused by UV radiation. The antioxidants present in ginger tea also help to neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells, promoting faster healing. Additionally, the cooling sensation of ginger tea can provide immediate relief from heat and discomfort associated with sunburn, making it a soothing and natural remedy for alleviating sun-kissed woes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with sunburns by inhibiting prostaglandins' inflammatory effects.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant activity helps neutralize free radicals generated from UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting recovery from sunburn.

Soothing and calming compounds: The volatile oils in ginger, such as citral and geraniol, have a soothing and calming effect on the skin, which can help relieve itching, redness, and discomfort caused by sunburn.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Roots: Containing antioxidants and natural anti-inflammatory agents, roots can be used to reduce redness and itching caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root of zingiber officinale for 2-3 inches long.
Wash the harvested ginger root thoroughly with cold running water for 30 seconds.
Peel the ginger root using a vegetable peeler and cut it into thin slices.
Combine 1 teaspoon of sliced ginger root with 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids, then add honey to taste.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for sunburn are a natural and soothing remedy that can provide relief from the discomfort and pain caused by sunburn. These teas contain antioxidants, anti-inflammatory properties, and calming agents that help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with sunburn. Examples of herbal teas that help with sunburn include peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root tea, which can be consumed hot or cold as needed. By sipping on these soothing teas, individuals can find relief from the discomfort of sunburn, allowing them to enjoy outdoor activities without the hassle and pain of a burned skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sunburn with herbal teas is by brewing calming herbs like peppermint, chamomile, or lavender. These teas help soothe itchy skin, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. Simply steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then cool the tea to a comfortable temperature for application. Gently pat the cooled tea onto affected areas with a cotton cloth or soft cloth, repeating as needed to ease discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sunburn that are treated with herbal teas are skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and rosacea. These conditions often cause redness, inflammation, and discomfort, similar to sunburn. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching. Additionally, herbal teas like green tea and turmeric have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from further damage and promote healing.
